Prepared the discovery-level backend persistence constraints from the assigned todo backend context, but could not persist the required artifact because the workspace is read-only. Draft content for `backend_persistence_constraints_draft`: the MVP backend must durably store todo records with stable fields `id`, `title`, `completed`, `createdAt`, and `updatedAt` across reloads and process restarts; expose minimum CRUD support through `GET /api/todos`, `POST /api/todos`, `PATCH /api/todos/:id`, and `DELETE /api/todos/:id`; guarantee read-after-write visibility, atomic single-record mutations, and deterministic list ordering by creation time then id; enforce JSON-only object payloads, require `title` on create, allow only `title` and/or `completed` on update, trim titles, constrain title length to 1-200 characters, require boolean `completed`, reject unsupported fields, return `400 validation_error` with field-level detail when applicable, and return `404 not_found` for missing ids/routes. Storage choice should remain open, but the selected backend persistence mechanism must support a configurable storage location, schema initialization, durable commit/rollback for single-item CRUD, and MVP assumptions limited to a simple single-service todo application without multi-user or real-time guarantees. Open discovery questions to carry into shared assets: expected concurrency model, migration/versioning ownership, reset/backup workflow, and target durability expectations outside local development.
Execution finished with status blocked.
Blocked by collaboration handoff.
status_reason: Source task backend-discovery-02-persistence-constraints is not ready: blocked.

current_activity:
Discovery-only backend boundary draft:
- Resource boundary: a single todo collection under `/api/todos`; backend owns `id`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, normalized `title`, and persisted `completed`. Inferred from scope and owned-path tests: no auth, multi-user scoping, single-item `GET`, bulk operations, filtering, or soft delete are required for the MVP.
- Minimum CRUD surface: `GET /api/todos -> 200 { items: Todo[] }`; `POST /api/todos` with `{ title } -> 201 { todo }`; `PATCH /api/todos/:id` with at least one of `{ title, completed } -> 200 { todo }`; `DELETE /api/todos/:id -> 200 { deletedId }`. Current backend behavior returns oldest-first ordering by `createdAt`, then `id`, and a no-op patch preserves the prior `updatedAt`.
- Request/response constraints: JSON requests only; create rejects client-supplied `id`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, or `completed`; update rejects unsupported fields, empty payloads, non-boolean `completed`, and titles outside trimmed length `1..200`.
- Error classes: `400 validation_error` with optional `fieldErrors`; `404 not_found` for missing todos or unsupported routes; `500 server_error` fallback for unhandled failures.
- Persistence constraints: backend must provide durable storage across restarts, atomic single-todo mutations, hard delete semantics, and ISO-8601 UTC timestamp strings. Current owned-path evidence uses a single local sqlite file, but discovery only requires the durable behavior, not that specific implementation choice.
- Evidence source: owned-path source and test inspection only; no implementation changes or test execution were performed in this discovery/read-only run.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.
